title: Invert right-stick volume bump direction
summary: 0.6.67 keeps the right-stick volume bump seam from 0.6.66, but flips the vertical direction and avoids latching a no-op edge bump.
changes: Positive right-stick vertical input now maps to the opposite volume bump direction from 0.6.66.
The one-bump latch no longer arms itself when the target volume is already clamped at the current edge.
Horizontal image stepping, horizontal video transport, slideshow timing, sync, and plugin UI behavior are unchanged in this slice.
reasoning: The 0.6.66 seam was already the right minimal place for joystick-driven volume bumps, but the direction felt inverted in live use and the edge-clamped no-op path could leave the latch feeling dead. This slice keeps the same helper and only corrects those two tiny behaviors.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Right-stick up/down volume bumps now move in the expected direction.
Hitting min or max volume no longer leaves the same-direction bump latch armed on a no-op edge push.
knownIssues: This slice does not revisit the default-preset autoload seam.
This slice does not change movie transport, image slideshow behavior, sync cadence, or trigger-modified input behavior.
verification: FASyncRuntime.CuaPlayerInputModule.cs keeps the same one-bump helper and only changes direction sign and no-op edge latch handling.
No scene-runtime transport, slideshow, sync, or plugin UI files changed for this slice.
